objective: Initialize Git-based sync transport between Windows Main and Ubuntu Worker for the MIDE pilot_v1 workflow
required_validation: A shared Git remote repo exists (GitHub or equivalent) accessible from both machines
Windows Main has the repo cloned or initialized with remote set
Ubuntu Worker has the repo cloned with remote set
Ubuntu Worker can push a test commit to the shared remote
Windows Main can pull that commit and confirm receipt
MIDE/pilot_v1 folder structure is present in the shared repo

phase_a_windows_main.description: Main sets up the shared remote
steps: Create a new private GitHub repository named 'mide-pilot' (or equivalent)
In C:\AI Assistant\MIDE\, run: git init
git commit -m 'main: initial MIDE pilot_v1 scaffold'
Record the GitHub repo URL in this result file

phase_b_ubuntu_worker.description: Worker clones the shared repo and confirms read access

phase_c_windows_main.description: Main pulls worker result and approves
steps: In C:\AI Assistant\MIDE\, run: git pull

execution_instructions: Phase A runs on Windows Main first
Phase B runs on Ubuntu Worker after Main pushes
Phase C runs on Windows Main after Worker pushes result
Use a private GitHub repo — do not make public
Do not include sensitive files (ngrok tokens, .env) in the repo
